name: Route of the Mills from Mingorría
name_original: Río Adaja y Molino Ituero — ida y vuelta desde Mingorría
description: Explore the easy 3.6-mile Route of the Mills from Mingorría, a hike along the Adaja River past historic flour mills and granite formations.
region_intro: Hiking around Mingorría offers routes through a landscape characterized by open plains, gentle hills, and historical sites. The region features paths along natural areas like the Campo Azálvaro and routes exploring ancient settlements. Trails often follow river courses or lead to reservoirs, providing varied terrain for exploration. The elevation gains are generally moderate, making many routes accessible. Best hiking trails around Mingorría The most popular hiking route is Bernuy-Salinero from Ávila — round trip via the Campo Azálvaro Natural Path, an 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 28 minutes to complete, traversing open natural areas. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castro de las Cogotas Circular Trail, a moderate 7.4 miles (11.9 km) path. This route explores an ancient settlement, offering historical interest and views. Local hikers also love the Route of the Mills from Mingorría, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail leading through river valleys and historical mill sites, often completed in about 1 hour 36 minutes. Hiking around Mingorría is defined by open plains, gentle hills, and historical sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy hikes to more moderate challenges. The routes in Mingorría are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Mingorría's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Mingorría?
A: There are over 15 hiking trails around Mingorría, offering a variety of experiences. These include 6 easy routes and 10 moderate trails, ensuring options for different skill levels.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Mingorría?
A: The hiking trails in Mingorría are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often appreciate the region's open plains, gentle hills, and the historical sites encountered along the paths.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Mingorría?
A: Yes, Mingorría offers 6 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1137567416/route-of-the-mills-from-mingorria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Mills from Mingorría&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.6 miles (5.8 km) long and takes about 1 hour 36 minutes, leading through river valleys and past historical mill sites.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes around Mingorría?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1325244174/castro-de-las-cogotas-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de las Cogotas Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.4 miles (11.9 km) path that explores an ancient settlement. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1325300979/la-presa-del-embalse-to-castro-de-las-cogotas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Presa del Embalse to Castro de las Cogotas — circular&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.3 miles (5.3 km) route.

Q: What kind of landscapes or viewpoints can I expect on Mingorría hikes?
A: Hiking around Mingorría offers a landscape characterized by open plains, gentle hills, and natural areas like the Campo Azálvaro. Many trails follow river courses or lead to reservoirs, providing varied terrain and scenic views of the Castilian countryside.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions to see while hiking?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1325244174/castro-de-las-cogotas-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de las Cogotas Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; explores an ancient settlement. Nearby, you can also visit historical landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4435034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ávila City Walls&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4435039" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza del Mercado Chico&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5797464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Roman bridge over the Adaja River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Mingorría?
A: Many trails in the Mingorría region are suitable for dogs, especially those traversing open natural areas. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, particularly in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. Always check local signage for specific restrictions.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Mingorría?
A: Yes, Mingorría has several easy routes that are suitable for families. These trails often feature moderate elevation gains and well-defined paths, making them enjoyable for all ages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1137567416/route-of-the-mills-from-mingorria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Mills from Mingorría&lt;/a&gt; is a good example of a shorter, accessible option.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Mingorría?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking in Mingorría, with mild temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are advisable. Winter offers a different charm, though some paths might be colder or occasionally damp.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Mingorría?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many routes starting from villages or popular attractions like the Castro de las Cogotas will have designated parking areas. For more remote trailheads, roadside parking might be an option, but it's always best to check specific route details or local maps beforehand.

Q: How can I get to the hiking trails in Mingorría using public transport?
A: While some trails might be accessible from larger towns like Ávila via regional bus services, direct public transport to all trailheads in Mingorría can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes connecting to Mingorría or nearby villages, and plan your hike accordingly, possibly starting from a town center.
tips: Ruins of the Mill and beautiful views of the Adaja River.

The place name Mingorría retains Basque reminiscences, deriving from “mendi gorria,” which means “red mountain” or “red land.” This is observed in other cases in the province of Ávila, such as the town of Mendigorría in Navarra. In historical documents, Mingorría already appears as Ningorría, and it is believed that there was an assimilation due to the change of the liquid initials “M” to “N.” Another legend attributes the name of Mingorría to the notice that José Chinchurreta gave to monarch Felipe II, justifying the delay in the supply of granite during the works of the Monastery of El Escorial.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Route of the Mills from Mingorría?
faqs.answer: The 5.76 km out-and-back section to the Río Adaja and Molino Ituero is generally considered easy, suitable for all fitness levels, with an elevation gain of about 110 meters. However, other sections of the broader Route of the Mills, particularly a 9.98 km circular route from Mingorría that includes the Molino de Trevejo, can feature pronounced slopes and delicate paths, especially after rain, making them moderately challenging.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. You'll encounter traditional bridle paths and tracks, some of which are accessible by car or bicycle. Other sections, especially those leading to the mills, are only passable on foot. The landscape features impressive granite rock formations known as 'berrocales' and diverse vegetation, including holm oak and pine forests.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Route of the Mills?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in historical and natural sights. You can explore the numerous ancient flour mills, many dating back to the 13th century, which are central to the region's history. Key highlights include the Ruins of the Ituero Mill and View of the Adaja River. The trail also offers extraordinary granite outcrops, lush forests, and panoramic viewpoints, sometimes revealing vultures soaring amongst the 'berrocales'.

faqs.question: Is the Route of the Mills from Mingorría suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 5.76 km out-and-back section to the Río Adaja and Molino Ituero is categorized as an easy hike, making it suitable for families. However, be aware that other parts of the broader route may have more challenging sections with pronounced slopes.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural surroundings with your pet. However, always ensure your dog is under control, especially in areas where you might encounter wildlife or other hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. During these seasons, the vegetation is lush, and the weather is ideal for hiking. Summer can be hot, and some sections might be delicate after heavy rainfall in winter or spring.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Mingorría?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from the village of Mingorría. Parking is typically available within or near the village, which serves as a convenient starting point for the route. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked or can be found in the village center.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to hike the Route of the Mills?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Route of the Mills from Mingorría. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What makes this trail unique or historically significant?
faqs.answer: The trail's primary draw is its rich historical significance, particularly the numerous ancient flour mills, many dating back to the 13th century. These mills represent a crucial part of the region's industrial past. The route offers a unique opportunity to delve into the history of these hydraulic structures and the traditional profession of the miller, set against a backdrop of beautiful natural scenery along the Adaja River.

faqs.question: Are there any local services or amenities available near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Mingorría, a village where you can typically find basic services such as cafes, restaurants, or small shops. It's advisable to check opening hours, especially if visiting during off-peak seasons.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or considerations I should be aware of on the trail?
faqs.answer: While parts of the trail are easy, some sections, particularly those leading to certain mills, can have pronounced slopes and become delicate after rainfall. Property owners have also installed new fences and wire fences in some areas, which might occasionally necessitate finding alternative routes. Always pay attention to signage and be prepared for varied conditions.

waypoints.name: Ruins of the Ituero Mill and View of the Adaja River
